title: Korean-Style Pork Mandu Buns
description: A twist on traditional Korean mandu, these steamed buns are filled with slow-cooked pork shoulder and served with a side of spicy Korean chili flakes.
instructions: In a large pot, combine pork shoulder, mild Korean chili flakes, and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 hours.
Preheat a steamer basket with a paper towel.
Combine Korean rice flour and water to form a dough. Knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball.
Place a piece of mandu in each dough ball, and fold the dough over to seal.
Brush with sesame oil and garnish with chopped green onion.
Serve with a side of spicy Korean chili flakes for added heat.
